Patch Set 2:

For some reason, we can't find the osd_object_sync_delay_us parameter on the MDS. In the test suite log, we see:

CMD: onyx-45vm9 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n osd-zfs.*.osd_object_sync_delay_us
onyx-45vm9: error: get_param: param_path 'osd-zfs/*/osd_object_sync_delay_us': No such file or directory
CMD: onyx-45vm10,onyx-45vm9 /usr/sbin/lctl set_param osd-zfs.*.osd_object_sync_delay_us=0
onyx-45vm9: error: set_param: param_path 'osd-zfs/*/osd_object_sync_delay_us': No such file or directory
onyx-45vm10: error: set_param: param_path 'osd-zfs/*/osd_object_sync_delay_us': No such file or directory

This parameter landed to master in January and thus, should be seen in this release.

parents.subject: LU-12671 mdd: rename mdd/sync_perm to sync_permissions

commit.subject: LU-11170 tests: add delay sync for sanity test 415
commit.message:
LU-11170 tests: add delay sync for sanity test 415

For a Lustre file system with ZFS targets and DNE configured,
renaming files, sanity test 415, takes more time than expected.
We should set delayed sync for ZFS for this test to see if
this has an impact on the amount of time to rename files.

Test-Parameters: trivial mdscount=2 mdtcount=2 fstype=zfs testlist=sanity
Test-Parameters: mdscount=2 mdtcount=2 fstype=zfs testlist=sanity
Signed-off-by: James Nunez <jnunez@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I98c61d43ca2813915e3590ecd0b011f768015ea4
Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nclark@whamcloud.com>
